page.title: Marbella Boat Rental Weather in September: Late Summer Sailing

data.meta_description: Plan your marbella boat rental weather september with sea temps of 22°C, 11h sun, and calm winds. Book your late-summer charter in Puerto Banús today.
data.summary: September in Marbella offers ideal conditions for boat charters with sea temperatures of 22°C, 11 hours of daily sunshine, and light Levante winds. This guide covers weather expectations, sea conditions, and best boats for late-summer sailing from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Average September air temperature: 27°C, sea temperature: 22°C
11 hours of daily sunshine, minimal rain (8mm total for the month)
Light Levante winds: 10–15 knots, ideal for smooth cruising
Sea state: gentle swell, rarely above Force 3–4
Peak season ends mid-September; fewer crowds, better availability
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and IVA
Sunset shifts from 20:40 to 19:50 by month's end

faq.q: Is September a good month for a boat charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, September is one of the best months. Air temperatures average 27°C, sea temperatures stay at 22°C, and you get 11 hours of sunshine daily. The water is still warm for swimming, and the winds are light, making for smooth sailing.

faq.q: What is the sea temperature in Marbella in September?
faq.a: The Mediterranean sea temperature in Marbella during September averages 22°C. This is warm enough for comfortable swimming, snorkelling, and water sports without a wetsuit.

faq.q: How windy is it in Marbella in September?
faq.a: September sees light Levante winds typically between 10 and 15 knots. The sea state is generally calm with a gentle swell, rarely exceeding Force 3–4. This is ideal for both motor yachts and sailing catamarans.

faq.q: Does it rain in Marbella in September?
faq.a: September is very dry in Marbella, with only about 8mm of rainfall for the whole month. You can expect clear skies and sunshine on the vast majority of days.

faq.q: What should I wear for a boat trip in Marbella in September?
faq.a: Light clothing, swimwear, a sun hat, and sunglasses are essential. Evenings can be cooler after sunset (around 19°C), so bring a light jacket or jumper. Sunscreen with high SPF is a must.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a September charter in Marbella?
faq.a: For small groups, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are great value at €749 for 2 hours. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers space and a free jet ski. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Can I go jet skiing in September in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, the sea conditions in September are perfect for jet skiing. Our Tier B charters on the Mangusta 80 (including grey and white variants) include a free jet ski with the minimum 4-hour booking.

faq.q: What time does the sun set in Marbella in September?
faq.a: Sunset in Marbella shifts from around 20:40 at the start of September to 19:50 by the end of the month. This gives you plenty of daylight for afternoon and early evening charters.

data.body_html:
September in Marbella is a sweet spot for late-summer sailing. The crowds thin out, the sea stays warm at 22°C, and you still get 11 hours of sunshine a day. If you're planning a marbella boat rental weather september is your best chance for calm seas, light winds, and reliable blue skies. This guide breaks down exactly what to expect and how to choose the right charter from Puerto Banús.

September Weather Overview: What to Expect

Average air temperatures sit at a comfortable 27°C, peaking at 30°C in early afternoon. Sea temperatures hold at 22°C, warm enough for swimming without a wetsuit. Rainfall is minimal — just 8mm across the entire month. The dominant wind is the Levante, blowing from the east at 10–15 knots, keeping the sea state gentle (Force 3–4). You'll get roughly 11 hours of daylight, with sunset moving from 20:40 to 19:50 as the month progresses.

Why September is Ideal for Boat Rentals

Peak tourist season winds down after mid-September, meaning fewer boats on the water and more availability at the marina. Sea conditions are stable, with minimal swell — perfect for families, couples, or groups who want a smooth ride. The water is still warm for snorkelling at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. You also get the benefit of lighter winds compared to July and August, making for a more relaxing cruise along the Costa del Sol.

Best Routes for a September Charter

From Puerto Banús, head east to Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile, or west towards Estepona and Sotogrande. A popular 4-hour route covers Cabopino and the dunes, while an 8-hour charter lets you reach the quieter coves near Cala del Faro. The sea state in September makes these runs comfortable on any of our fleet. For a shorter trip, the 2-hour Tier A charter on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 gives you a taste of the coast without committing a full day.

Which Boats Work Best for September Weather

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and IVA. For small groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are cost-effective at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) — available in grey and white — offers a free jet ski with a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. Other boats like the Pershing 46 or Ferretti 94 have pricing on request via WhatsApp. The calm September seas suit both motor yachts and the Lagoon 380 catamaran.

What's Included in Every Charter

Every booking comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Onboard you'll find water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There are no hidden fees. Our Tier B charters (Mangusta 80 variants) also include a jet ski for free. For specific pricing on other boats, just WhatsApp us.

Packing Tips for a September Boat Trip

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunglasses, and high-SPF sunscreen. A light jacket or jumper is useful for the return leg after sunset, when temperatures drop to around 19°C. Non-slip shoes are recommended for moving around the deck. If you're prone to seasickness, note that September seas are generally calm, but a patch or tablet can give peace of mind.

Booking Considerations for September

Availability is better than in August, but weekends still fill up quickly. Book at least a week in advance for the best choice of boats. The Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) are popular for short trips, while the Mangusta 80 is a favourite for all-day charters. For a full list of options, browse our fleet and see what suits your group size and budget.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is September a good month for a boat charter in Marbella?

Yes, September is one of the best months. Air temperatures average 27°C, sea temperatures stay at 22°C, and you get 11 hours of sunshine daily. The water is still warm for swimming, and the winds are light, making for smooth sailing.

What is the sea temperature in Marbella in September?

The Mediterranean sea temperature in Marbella during September averages 22°C. This is warm enough for comfortable swimming, snorkelling, and water sports without a wetsuit.

How windy is it in Marbella in September?

September sees light Levante winds typically between 10 and 15 knots. The sea state is generally calm with a gentle swell, rarely exceeding Force 3–4. This is ideal for both motor yachts and sailing catamarans.

Does it rain in Marbella in September?

September is very dry in Marbella, with only about 8mm of rainfall for the whole month. You can expect clear skies and sunshine on the vast majority of days.

What should I wear for a boat trip in Marbella in September?

Light clothing, swimwear, a sun hat, and sunglasses are essential. Evenings can be cooler after sunset (around 19°C), so bring a light jacket or jumper. Sunscreen with high SPF is a must.

Which boats are best for a September charter in Marbella?

For small groups, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 are great value at €749 for 2 hours. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers space and a free jet ski. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Can I go jet skiing in September in Marbella?

Yes, the sea conditions in September are perfect for jet skiing. Our Tier B charters on the Mangusta 80 (including grey and white variants) include a free jet ski with the minimum 4-hour booking.

What time does the sun set in Marbella in September?

Sunset in Marbella shifts from around 20:40 at the start of September to 19:50 by the end of the month. This gives you plenty of daylight for afternoon and early evening charters.
